comment.body: Tariffs are so versatile. They have the power to cause inflation directly; and the power to induce a recession indirectly (cost-saving headcount reductions to offset higher input expenses)
comment.raw_body: Tariffs are so versatile. They have the power to cause inflation directly; and the power to induce a recession indirectly (cost-saving headcount reductions to offset higher input expenses)

comment.body:
Alma didn’t write much about this..she only hinted i think, but it’s important whether the Fed cuts because inflation risk is easing or because recession risk has spiked. In the latter case, rate cuts won’t help because of tariffs, and that would be the most bearish QE you’ve probably ever seen.
This is especially important for those planning long term. It’s no accident there’s no clear signal from alma or anyone else. In times like this, most money flows into precious metals and bonds. US equity outflow is high

comment.body: I’ll add that it was very telling that the market sold off on the weak NFP, and then stabilized lower, as you can see, rather than rallying. It didn’t cheer bad news. A stagflationary environment is a trap for the central bank. Inflation has to be treated separately from economic growth, something the administration deliberately avoids. That’s what alma means when she says it’s enough for trump if growth is only on paper. A hawkish cut will bring strong bearish pressure if the labor data continue to weaken

comment.body:
This is an amateurish guess, but I think both cpi and ppi comes in a bit hotter.
The market goes down to maybe the weekly measured move and then dip buyers saves it. We continue to go up till Friday and as Alma said the buyback will come early next week too. 
But then vixperation comes and I’m no option guy, but it has happen many times that the spot can change direction around that date. 
Then comes Powell on Jackson hole and he won’t deliver what the market wants him to say. And that could trigger a sell off.

comment.body: So Putin will try to get as much as he can, it will be bad if Trump unilaterally sells out allies in Europe... or more likely Trump will say "we will see, it's up to Ukraine and the EU." comment.body:
Hope you had a good weekend Alma, quick question about the OU process notes you had posted in the Q&A channel a while back. 

I’m working on optimizing the parameters currently and from what I understand the rough/fractional OU process is non-Markovian and uses scale-by-h^H instead of the OU equilibrium std. Why would you suggest a non-Markovian process when it integrates with the HMM module of the system? Or is that already covered by that module? 

Why would you want the “rougher” version over the standard model? Having a tough time wrapping my head around that part of the math and its relation to the OU module right now
